title: Infosys Science Foundation Honors Infosys Prize 2025 Laureates
detailed_summary: Infosys Science Foundation awarded the Infosys Prize 2025 in Bengaluru on January 10, 2026, recognizing six researchers across Economics, Engineering and Computer Science, Humanities and Social Sciences, Life Sciences, Mathematical Sciences, and Physical Sciences. Each prize consisted of a pure gold medal, a citation, and a prize purse of USD 100,000.

key_facts.fact: The Infosys Science Foundation awarded the winners of the Infosys Prize 2025 at a felicitation ceremony in Bengaluru on January 10, 2026.
key_facts.section_heading: PRESS RELEASE Infosys Science Foundation Honors Outstanding Researchers with the Infosys Prize 2025

key_facts.fact: The prize recognized researchers under the age of 40 across six categories: Economics, Engineering and Computer Science, Humanities and Social Sciences, Life Sciences, Mathematical Sciences, and Physical Sciences.
key_facts.section_heading: PRESS RELEASE Infosys Science Foundation Honors Outstanding Researchers with the Infosys Prize 2025

key_facts.fact: Each winner received a pure gold medal, a citation, and a prize purse of USD 100,000.
key_facts.section_heading: PRESS RELEASE Infosys Science Foundation Honors Outstanding Researchers with the Infosys Prize 2025

esg_or_csr_items.item: Infosys Science Foundation awarded the Infosys Prize 2025 to six researchers, with each prize consisting of a pure gold medal, a citation, and a prize purse of USD 100,000.
esg_or_csr_items.section_heading: PRESS RELEASE Infosys Science Foundation Honors Outstanding Researchers with the Infosys Prize 2025

important_text_spans.text: The Infosys Science Foundation (ISF) today awarded the winners of the Infosys Prize 2025 at a felicitation ceremony in Bengaluru.
important_text_spans.section_heading: PRESS RELEASE Infosys Science Foundation Honors Outstanding Researchers with the Infosys Prize 2025
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the main event described in the document.

important_text_spans.text: The winners were felicitated by Chief Guest Prof. Randy Schekman, 2013 Nobel laureate in Physiology /Medicine, Professor in the Department of Molecular and Cell Biology, University of California, Berkeley, and Investigator of the Howard Hughes Medical Institute, with a pure gold medal, a citation, and a prize purse of USD 100,000.
important_text_spans.section_heading: PRESS RELEASE Infosys Science Foundation Honors Outstanding Researchers with the Infosys Prize 2025
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the prize scale and award components.
